name: eval-5-connection-validation
prompt: Using Rete.js v2, create a connection validation system that blocks incompatible socket types. Set up three node types: NumberNode (outputs a 'number' socket), StringNode (accepts a 'string' input), and MixedNode (accepts both). Add a pipe via editor.addPipe() that intercepts 'connectioncreate' events and blocks NumberNode→StringNode connections. Return undefined to block, return context to allow. Output one file: connection-validation.ts
assertions: uses-add-pipe: uses editor.addPipe() (not editor.on(), which is Rete v1 API)
returns-undefined-to-block: returns undefined (not false or throw) to cancel the connection
uses-editor-get-node: calls editor.getNode() to get node instances for type checking
returns-context-to-allow: returns context to permit valid connections through
